programs.title: Nazi Megastructures
programs.desc: In their quest for world domination, during World War II, Germany constructed advanced military equipment, created deadly technologies, and built powerful defensive fortifications.Engineers working for the Nazis designed modern weapons, including those of mass destruction.The authors of the program present the most ambitious military projects.They tell the story of people working on weapons and other strategic inventions that were supposed to ensure the victory of the Third Reich.Although the Nazis ultimately lost the war, the military technologies created by their scientists proved groundbreaking.

programs.title: Air Crash Investigation 21
programs.desc: Reconstructions of real plane crashes.By recreating the course of events, the program's creators try to understand what led to the unfortunate events.They recreate the circumstances in which the tragedy occurred: the weather, the atmosphere on board the plane, the decisions and actions of the pilots.They also show the work of people investigating the causes of disasters and conduct a computer flight simulation.Viewers can hear various comments from specialists and testimonies of witnesses to these events.The creators of the program will prove that air accidents are often caused by human slowness and lack of imagination.Each disaster is thoroughly analyzed and the lessons learned help minimize the number of tragedies in the future.

programs.title: Mayday 13
programs.desc: The series' creators solve the mysteries of the biggest plane crashes.Trying to recreate the events that took place before the tragedies, they reconstruct the preceding situations on board the machines.

programs.title: Air Crash Investigation
programs.desc: The series' creators solve the mysteries of the biggest plane crashes.Trying to recreate the events that took place before the tragedies, they reconstruct the preceding situations on board the machines.

programs.title: Air Crash Investigation Special Report 3
programs.desc: Analysis of the most tragic accidents in the history of aviation.The investigation is based on reconstruction of events, photographic materials, accounts of survivors and rescuers, and computer graphics.
